title: Security intensified in Delhi ahead of Parliament session, CJP’s march call
author: PTI
content_html:
Security has been further intensified across the New Delhi district ahead of the Monsoon session of Parliament and the Cockroach Janta Party’s (CJP) proposed “Chalo Sansad” march.

Also read: Jantar Mantar protest updates on July 19, 2026

The Delhi Police has deployed additional personnel, strengthened barricading and is conducting intensive vehicle checks at key entry points, sources said on Sunday (July 19, 2026).

Preventive deployment will remain in place throughout the Parliament session, police added.

Several entry points have been converted into high-security zones with multi-layered barricades, enhanced surveillance, anti-sabotage checks and round-the-clock patrolling in New Delhi, Central Delhi and parts of North Delhi, they said.

Related Stories

  • ‘There was no need for Govt. intervention’: Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali on Delhi HC refusing interim relief on hospital transfer

  • Delhi HC declines interim relief on plea by Wangchuk’s wife seeking transfer from Safdarjung Hospital

  • Sonam Wangchuk calls July 20 Parliament march India's 'second freedom movement'

A senior police source said elaborate preventive arrangements have been made to ensure law and order during the Parliament session.

“Every vehicle entering the New Delhi district is being thoroughly checked. Additional pickets have been set up at strategic locations, and reserve forces have been kept on standby. CCTV surveillance has also been intensified across sensitive areas,” the source said.

Security has been strengthened around Jantar Mantar, Parliament Street, Central Vista, Connaught Place, Shankar Chowk and other key installations, while rapid response teams have been positioned to respond to any contingency.

The heightened vigil comes amid fresh developments at the protest site, where CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke continued his indefinite hunger strike after activist Sonam Wangchuk was whisked away on Saturday (July 18, 2026) to the Safdarjung Hospital.

Mr. Wangchuk issued a message from the hospital urging supporters to participate in the proposed July 20 march.

Published - July 19, 2026 05:45 pm IST

title: ‘There was no need for Govt. intervention’: Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali on Delhi HC refusing interim relief on hospital transfer
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Soon after the Delhi High Court on Sunday (July 19, 2026) refused to interfere with activist Sonam Wangchuk’s treatment at Safdarjung Hospital, his wife Gitanjali Angmo said the petition was “wrongly interpreted” and questioned why the government, if it had to intervene, couldn’t do so “democratically with the family’s trust”.

On Saturday (July 1, 2026), Mr. Wangchuk was taken forcibly to Safdarjung Hospital by the Delhi Police on day 21 of his hunger strike.

His health is fine and there was no need for government intervention, his wife said, speaking to reporters after the hearing. “If a man is fasting - and today is his 22nd day - he has fasted 35 days in Ladakh. His health is fine. There was no need for government intervention”.

She said they would be appealing on Monday (July 20, 2026), “to rectify the errors in the order today”.

Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE updates - July 19, 2026

Ms. Angmo asserted that Mr. Wangchuk should have free access to his doctors, adding that they went to the high court with the petition “that a free Indian citizen has the liberty to be treated where he wishes by doctors he’s comfortable with”. She said that a letter was submitted to the court prior to the hearing, but they are still awaiting a response.

“He [Mr. Wangchuk] should have free access. His doctors should meet him. So this is a larger question to answer, does a citizen not have the right to access care of his choice? And if the government intervenes, can’t they do so democratically with family’s trust?” Mr. Angmo asked.

Earlier in court, Justice Mini Pushkarna said no interim order was required at this stage on a petition by Ms. Angmo, seeking permission to shift him to a private facility. The petition will be heard next on July 24.

“The high court has not allowed it because they said they’re worried about his health. We submitted that they can send their doctors wherever he’s being treated,” Ms. Angmo said.

In a special hearing, Justice Pushkarna said Safdarjung doctors were closely monitoring the fasting activist, and it could not be said that any force was being used against him in violation of his bodily integrity. The judge also said the government’s removal of Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar to Safdarjung Hospital cannot be called arbitrary.

Later, Ms. Angmo said that they wanted to shift Mr. Wangchuk to Medanta or any other hospital in the national capital. “This is fundamental right. We were exercising right of our comfort and choice. There’s a trust deficit in this country. People don’t trust government institutions.”

“Is India truly democratic and free? We asked the judge, the way police is roaming the floor and take all our belongings and his tablet... He’s not in detention like he was in the Jodhpur jail. He should have free access. His doctors should meet him,” she added.

On current status of Wangchuk in hospital

Ms. Angmo said the High Court did not allow the hospital transfer saying that they are worried about the activist’s health. “His physician has no access. There’s obstruction everywhere. We asked for a sample to get a 2.9 potassium reading from a different test centre..if 2.9 is so life threatening, then they should have given us the sample far earlier,” she said. 

“For 20 days when we were checking [Wangchuk’s health] every day, I got reports constantly. But since we got here we aren’t getting reports. The court has noted our demand that we get reports immediately. We want to go to a hospital where he gets all these things,” she added.

Mr. Wangchuk is still continuing the fast, she said. “Sonam wanted that he wanted to continue the fast. They have been asking him to have fluids but he didn’t have. He’s having water.”

‘In Wangchuk’s absence, number of protesters will double’

“We know the government took him [Mr. Wangchuk] here [Safdarjung Hospital] because they were afraid that if he led a protest many people would come,” she said, asserting that “those numbers will double” in his absence. 

Ms. Angmo urged people to join the proposes ‘Chalo Sansad’ march on Monday (July 20, 2026). “May everyone join the march. Education is the foundation for the country. If we want to be a vishwaguru especially.”

“In no developed country do people get an education based on their economic status. The demographic dividend we had a decade back of 50% people below age of 25...if we leveraged that we would have a generation of leaders,” she said.

Students have shown that Gen-Z protests peacefully: Angmo

Ahead of the planned protest march, Ms. Angmo conveyed Mr. Wangchuk’s message to the public. “Sonam’s message to everyone is that even in his absence we must go with double the enthusiasm and four times the numbers.”

“The way he has been advocating people to demonstrate peacefully, I’ll tell everyone two things: march peacefully and watch out for nefarious disruptive elements. They will try to colour the protest. But over the last 22 days students have shown that Gen Z protests peacefully,” she added.

title: Naveen Patnaik asks Centre to initiate dialogue with students
author: Satyasundar Barik
content_html:
Former Odisha Chief Minister and Biju Janata Dal president Naveen Patnaik on Sunday (July 19, 2026) urged the Centre to initiate dialogue with students who have been protesting peacefully for bringing reforms in examination process and restoring faith of students in the system and take up the issue for debate in Parliament.

Also Read : Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE

“India too has made remarkable progress because of the strength of its education system. It has nurtured generations of brilliant doctors, scientists, engineers, teachers, and innovators who have shaped modern India. Today, however, trust in this system stands broken. The damage goes far beyond a failed exam,” Mr. Patnaik said, in a post on X.

Stating that for millions of children in India, education remains the only path to a brighter future and every developed nation is built upon a strong education system and a transparent, credible examination process, he said, “it (mismanagement of education system) shatters faith in the very foundation of education system. It tells deserving students that hard work no longer matters, and robs them of their only ladder out of poverty.”

For millions of children in our country, education remains the only path to a brighter future. Every developed nation is built upon a strong education system and a transparent, credible examination process. India too has made remarkable progress because of the strength of its…

— Naveen Patnaik (@Naveen_Odisha) July 19, 2026

“A nation that compromises the integrity of its examination system, compromises its own future. This grave issue, affecting millions of students across the country, must be taken up in Parliament as the highest priority,” said Mr. Patnaik.

“It deserves a thorough debate in the temple of our democracy, leading to concrete reforms that ensure paper leaks, flawed assessments, and systemic failures are never repeated. Accountability must also be fixed for this lapse of trust,” the veteran politician demanded.

“Politics aside, I urge the government to initiate a meaningful dialogue with the students who have been peacefully protesting for days. They must be given a platform to express their views. A vibrant democracy advances through dialogue, not silence and this dialogue is most vital when it involves the youth of the nation,” he pointed out.

“The children and young people of India are looking to us for leadership. We cannot fail them. Sincere, open-minded dialogue with the peaceful youth protestors will help restore faith in our democracy, in our education system, and in the leadership entrusted with shaping their future. The BJD stands firmly with the youth and students of the country,” said former CM.

Mr. Patnaik’s backing for students demanding resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and transparent education system has come at a time when activist Sonam Wangchuk, recuperating at Delhi’s Safdarjung hospital, continued to stick his earlier announcement of marching to Parliament when it resumes for the Monsoon Session on Monday. Mr. Wangchuk had been continuing hunger strike over the alleged NEET paper leak at Jantar Mantar before he was admitted to hospital. In Odisha, the BJD had staged multiple demonstrations demanding resignation of Mr. Pradhan.

title: NEET-UG: NTA warns against fake, AI-generated OMR sheets; legal action may follow
author: PTI
content_html:
The National Testing Agency (NTA) has issued an advisory to NEET-UG 2026 candidates and their parents, warning them not to submit fake or AI-generated OMR sheets when raising complaints about score discrepancies.

The warning comes after the NTA reviewed several complaints regarding differences between candidates' expected and declared NEET 2026 scores. During the verification process, the agency found that many of the OMR sheets submitted with complaints appeared to be fake or created using artificial intelligence (AI).

"NTA is closely monitoring and scrutinising all complaints. In the wake of many OMR sheets submitted for scrutiny turning out to be fake/AI-generated, students and parents are advised to submit only original OMRs for scrutiny. Any fake/AI-generated OMR may invite legal action against the complainant," the agency said in an advisory.

The results for the medical entrance were announced on July 16, with 11.21 lakh candidates qualifying the exam.

The NTA cancelled the May 3 exam on May 12 amid allegations of a paper leak; the CBI is investigating the matter, and the exam was re-conducted on June 21.

Following the controversy over the paper leak and alleged irregularities in the exam process, the Centre announced that the exam will now be conducted as a computer-based test (CBT) instead of pen-and-paper mode.

Detailed deliberations have been ongoing for several years between the education and health ministries on whether to conduct NEET-UG in pen-and-paper or online mode.

The National Eligibility cum Entrance Test-Undergraduate (NEET-UG) is the country's largest undergraduate entrance exam, with nearly 25 lakh candidates registering annually.

The NTA conducts the test every year for admission into medical colleges, where 108,000 seats are available for the MBBS course. Of these, approximately 56,000 are in government hospitals and about 52,000 are in private colleges. Undergraduate courses in dentistry, Ayurveda, Unani and Siddha also utilise NEET results for admissions.

title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE: PIL interpreted wrongly; Sonam's health is fine, there was no need for Govt intervention, says Gitanjali
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Key Updates
  • There was no need for government intervention, Sonam's health is fine: Gitanjali Angmo
  • Wangchuk's physician has no access, there's obstruction everywhere: Gitanjali Angm
  • Delhi HC agrees to urgently hear plea by Wangchuk’s wife to shift activist from Safdarjung Hospital
  • Sonam Wangchuk's wife to approach Delhi HC to shift activist to different hospital
  • Sonam Wangchuk calls on students to join march to Parliament
Delhi HC declines interim relief on plea by Wangchuk's wife seeking transfer from Safdarjung Hospital

Delhi HC declined interim relief on the plea by Sonam Wangchuk’s wife seeking his transfer from Safdarjung Hospital to a hospital of his choice. 

The Court noted that Mr. Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for 17–18 days and that his medical condition is “perilous.” 

It accepted the Centre’s stand that shifting him to Safdarjung was a medical decision, not an arbitrary one. The Court recorded that his potassium levels are dangerously low, medicines have been administered with his consent, IV fluids have not been given as he has not consented.

-Soibam Rocky Singh

  • July 19, 2026 19:11
    Delhi Traffic issues advisory ahead of Parliament monsoon session

    Ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament and the proposed ‘Chalo Sansad’ march by the Cockroach Janata Party, the Delhi Traffic Police on Sunday (July 19, 2026) issued an advisory warning of heavy vehicular movement and restrictions around the Parliament complex.

    As per the advisory, special traffic arrangements have been put in place in the New Delhi Range, with commuters being asked to plan their journeys and allow extra travel time.

    The roads likely to be affected are Rafi Marg, Motilal Nehru Marg, Maulana Azad Road, K Kamaraj Marg, Raisina Road, Rajendra Prasad Road, Parliament Street, Ashoka Road, Talkatora Road, Pandit Pant Marg and Rakab Ganj Gurudwara Road.

    Traffic police also advised motorists to avoid key junctions and roundabouts, including Vijay Chowk, Boat Club, Rail Bhawan roundabout, Sunehri Masjid roundabout, Patel Chowk roundabout, Boota Singh roundabout, Prime Chowk roundabout, GRG roundabout and Jalebi Chowk roundabout.

    - PTI

  • July 19, 2026 18:53
    Sisodia urges all parties to unite for Pradhan’s sacking

    Former Delhi Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia on Sunday (July 19, 2026) urged all political parties to join the protest for Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over the NEET paper leak.

    Addressing protesters at Jantar Mantar, Mr. Sisodia said education reforms need sustained efforts.

    “Education reform is not a small task. It takes years of consistent effort. I have spent my life working for education reforms, and I am ready to dedicate the rest of my life to this cause,” he said.

    Mr. Sisodia called the protesters at Jantar Mantar “soldiers of the education revolution” and said Abhijeet Dipke and his companions have taken up the fight against the “education mafia.” The Aam Aadmi Party leader said Sonam Wangchuk has strengthened the movement by putting his own health at stake and chastised the government for not yet speaking to the agitators.

    - PTI

  • July 19, 2026 18:12
    Civil rights group condemns ‘forcible removal’ of Sonam Wangchuk from protest site

    A civil rights organisation on Sunday (July 19, 2026) criticised the “forcible removal” of activist Sonam Wangchuk from his protest site by the Delhi Police and alleged that the action constitutes an attack on the democratic right to peaceful protest and freedom of expression.

    In a statement, Janhastakshep also claimed that students and young people across the country are deeply distressed by alleged corruption and the “exploitation” by coaching institutes.

    Parents are increasingly worried about their children’s education and future, and instead of addressing these concerns, the central and state governments continue to accelerate the privatisation of educational institutions and healthcare services, Janhastakshep convener Vikas Bajpai and co-convener Anil Dubey alleged in the statement.

    - PTI

  • July 19, 2026 18:06
    Fight should now be launched to change government that is not accountable: Uddhav

    Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray on Sunday (July 18, 2026) attacked the BJP-led Centre, saying a fight should now be launched to change a government that is not accountable.

    Addressing a rally in Mumbai, he accused the government of stealing the future of the youth, referring to alleged irregularities in the NEET exam and student suicides tied to it, which have prompted activist Sonam Wangchuk to launch a hunger strike.

    The criticism of the government by Mr. Thackeray, whose party recently saw six of its Lok Sabha MPs switching over to the rival Shiv Sena, led by Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde, came on the eve of Parliament’s monsoon session.

    “A fight should now be launched to change a government that is not accountable. And this fight is not just about Sonam Wangchuk,” he said.

    - PTI

title: Govt. constitutes expert panel to assess El Nino impact
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
The Telangana government constituted a 26-member State-level Expert Committee, headed by State Planning Board Vice-Chairman G. Chinna Reddy, to assess the likely impact of the El Nino phenomenon on the State and recommend measures to mitigate its effects.

According to the G.O. issued by the government, the committee comprises senior officials from various government departments and has been tasked with conducting a comprehensive study on the potential consequences of El Nino across sectors.

The panel will examine the possible impact of the weather phenomenon, evaluate the State’s preparedness and suggest strategies to minimise its adverse effects. It has been asked to submit a detailed report to the government after an in-depth assessment.

The government is expected to formulate its future course of action based on the committee’s recommendations, with the report likely to guide policy decisions relating to agriculture, water resources, disaster preparedness and other affected sectors.

title: Bandi Sanjay urges Centre to probe diversion of Telangana PR funds
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
Union Minister of State for Home Affairs Bandi Sanjay Kumar on Sunday urged Union Minister for Panchayati Raj Rajiv Ranjan Singh to conduct a special inquiry into the utilisation of 15th Finance Commission grants in Telangana, alleging that the funds were being diverted for purposes other than those permitted under the guidelines.

In a communication released to the media, Mr. Sanjay Kumar sought immediate directions to the State government to ensure that funds earmarked for village development are not used for staff salaries, electricity bills, routine administrative expenses or any other unauthorised purposes.

Mr. Sanjay Kumar alleged that a total of ₹2,293.74 crore had been released in phases and credited to the State Treasury so far, while another ₹729.41 crore remained due for release. He claimed that the funds were being utilised for staff salaries, electricity bills, routine maintenance and other administrative expenditures instead of developmental works.

He further alleged that gram panchayat sarpanches had expressed serious concern over being pressured to use the funds for such non-permissible purposes. The Union government should put in place a strict monitoring mechanism to ensure that funds released to gram panchayats are utilised exclusively for intended developmental works, he said.

He also urged the Centre to direct the State government to initiate disciplinary and legal action against officials responsible for diverting the funds. The Congress government should be asked to explain the alleged diversion of funds, Mr. Sanjay Kumar said in the letter.

title: Govt. constitutes single window committee for speedy clearances for affordable housing projects
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
The Telangana government has granted a 100% exemption from planning and building permission-related charges for the Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) and Low-Income Group (LIG) component of the Affordable Urban Housing Programme (Indiramma Urban Housing) being implemented by the Telangana Housing Board (TGHB).

The exemptions cover building permit fees, scrutiny and processing fees, development and betterment charges, layout approval and subdivision charges, land-use conversion charges, occupancy certificate fees, city-level infrastructure impact fees, fire service NOC-related fees, rainwater harvesting charges, road charges under Comprehensive Road Maintenance Programme and all other planning and building permission-related fees ordinarily levied by urban local bodies, metropolitan and other planning authorities.

The government has simultaneously constituted the TGHB Affordable Urban Housing Single Window Committee to provide time-bound approvals for projects under the programme. Headed by the Special Chief Secretary/Principal Secretary of the Municipal Administration and Urban Development department, the committee includes representatives from the Housing department, Telangana Housing Board, Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation, Cyberabad Municipal Corporation, Malkajgiri Municipal Corporation, Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority, Directorate of Town and Country Planning, Fire Services, water supply and sewerage utilities, power distribution companies, Telangana Pollution Control Board, TG RERA and other departments. District Collectors and local planning authorities will participate depending on the location of the project.

According to the government order, the committee will accept a common application through the BuildNow platform, issue building and layout permissions and no-objection certificates in a time-bound manner, communicate deficiencies through a single consolidated notice, grant deemed approvals wherever legally permissible, determine applicable and waived charges and coordinate with line departments to provide external infrastructure, including water supply, sewerage, roads, power and fire-safety connectivity up to the project boundary.

The order also extends to the Affordable Urban Housing Programme the special regulations and procedural relaxations that were earlier introduced for the State’s Double Bedroom Housing Scheme, subject to statutory safety and environmental requirements.

The exemptions will apply only to the EWS/LIG component of the programme implemented by the Telangana Housing Board. However, structural and fire safety, environmental and aviation clearances and other statutory safety requirements will continue to be mandatory wherever applicable. Statutory taxes, duties, cess and utility service charges will remain payable. The Director of Town and Country Planning has been directed to create a dedicated project category in the BuildNow platform for processing proposals under the scheme.
